Soul of Christ, sanctify me.
Body of Christ, save me.
Blood of Christ, inebriate me.
Water from the side of Christ, wash me.
Passion of Christ, strengthen me.
O Good Jesus, hear me.
Within Thy wounds, hide me.
Suffer me not to be separated from Thee.
From the malicious enemy, defend me.
In the hour of my death, call me.
And bid me come to Thee, that with Thy Saints
I may praise Thee for ever and ever. Amen.

Anima Christi Missionaries of Consolation

Together, We
Console Hearts

Founded in 2019 under the inspiration of the Holy Spirit, we are a missionary family — contemplative and active — drawing from the wellspring of the Anima Christi prayer and the Franciscan spirit of simplicity, peace, and joy.

Pray the Anima Christi Join the Mission

Scripture of the Day
"By His stripes, we are healed."
Isaiah 53:5
Who We Are

Mission & Charism

The Anima Christi Missionaries of Consolation were founded in 2019 through the inspiration of the Holy Spirit and the blessing of Bishop Peter Iornzuul Adoboh (of blessed memory), first Bishop of Katsina-Ala Diocese, together with our Foundress, Sr. Felicia Mbasen Agibi.

We are a contemplative-active missionary family rooted in the prayer Anima Christi and inspired by the spirit of Our Lady of Consolation, St. Francis of Assisi, and St. Clare of Assisi.

Our charism is consolation — to be living witnesses of Christ's merciful and healing love in a wounded world.

In a world often torn by violence, poverty, and brokenness, ACMC exists to remind every person: "By His stripes, we are healed" (Isaiah 53:5).

Our presence is a lantern of hope — assuring the world that no one suffers alone, and that Christ, the Consoler, walks with His people.

Console the Afflicted

Offering compassionate presence to those burdened by sorrow, trauma, or injustice.

Heal Wounds

Through prayer, accompaniment, and works of mercy — body, mind, and spirit.

Proclaim Peace

Forming communities of hope and reconciliation, where the Gospel is lived.

Live the Prayer

United to Christ crucified and risen, whose mercy is the desire of every heart.

2019
Founded by Sr. Felicia Mbasen Agibi under the inspiration of the Holy Spirit and the blessing of Bishop Peter Iornzuul Adoboh.
Mission Grows
ACMC expands its ministry to shelters, schools, hospitals, streets, and communities longing for peace across Nigeria.
Today
Under the guidance of Most Rev. Isaac B. Dugu, ACMC continues to walk with the wounded as instruments of Christ's healing love.
Community Hierarchy

His Holiness the Pope

Supreme Pontiff · Universal Church

As a Catholic missionary community, ACMC is united with the Holy Father and the universal Church, guided by his magisterium and pastoral care.

Most Rev. Isaac B. Dugu

Bishop · Principal Seat

Most Rev. Isaac B. Dugu serves as the Bishop of our principal seat, providing pastoral oversight and episcopal blessing to the ACMC community and its mission.

Sr. Felicia Mbasen Agibi

Foundress · ACMC

Sr. Felicia Mbasen Agibi, our Foundress, received the charism of consolation and, together with Bishop Adoboh, gave birth to the Anima Christi Missionaries of Consolation in 2019.
The Heart of Our Consecrated Life

Our Prayer

"Every woman religious must bear witness to the primacy of God and dedicate a sufficiently long period of time every day to stand before the Lord, to tell Him her love, and above all to let herself be loved by Him."

— Pope St. John Paul II

As consecrated women of consolation, we joyfully embrace our first and deepest calling: to live in constant union with Christ through prayer. In the Anima Christi we find both our identity and our mission — clinging to Christ who sanctifies, saves, and consoles.

Prayer is the wellspring of our consecrated life. No service, ministry, or work of mercy bears fruit unless it flows from intimacy with Christ, the Crucified and Risen Lord. "Apart from me you can do nothing" (Jn 15:5).

In prayer we discover who we truly are: poor and humble handmaids, clothed in the mercy and riches of our Divine Bridegroom. Our dependence on Him is as urgent as the breath we take, for in Him alone do we live and move and have our being.

Rooted in the Franciscan spirit of simplicity and joy, we weave prayer into the rhythm of each day — beginning with Christ, abiding with Christ, and ending in Christ. In this way, our lives become a seamless garment of His love, a living sign of consolation for the world.

Praise and Intercession

As Anima Christi Missionaries of Consolation, we follow the example of Saint Francis, who "became prayer" itself, living as women of praise. We joyfully offer God the worship and adoration often denied Him by a hurting and indifferent world.

Our praise is never private or isolated; it is always a prayer of intercession and reparation. We lift up those who do not pray, those who do not love, or those who may even reject God. Through praise and intercession, our lives become living acts of consolation.

Silence and Solitude

Silence, the faithful friend of all who seek holiness, is for us a sacred space where the consoling presence of Christ speaks most clearly. In silence, we enter more deeply into intimacy with our Beloved Spouse, grow in self-knowledge, and learn to discern the gentle stirrings of the Holy Spirit.

Fridays hold a special place in our rhythm of life. On this day, we accompany our fasting with deeper silence, interceding especially for the broken, the abandoned, and all who suffer. Saturdays invite us to move from contemplation to action — serving the homeless and the forgotten on the streets.

The Breath of Consolation

Liturgy of the Hours

"In Him we live and move and have our being" (Acts 17:28). We embrace the Liturgy of the Hours as a heartbeat of our vocation — through Morning and Evening Prayer, through the Psalms, Canticles, and intercessions, we allow the mystery of Christ to permeate and sanctify each moment of the day.

☀
5:30 – 7:00 AM
Morning Prayer
Rising with Christ, we consecrate the new day through Lauds — psalms, canticles, and morning intercessions.
Morning Rhythm:

Invitatory Psalm · Office of Readings · Lauds · Eucharist · Lectio Divina

"Let my morning prayer come before You; incline Your ear to my cry."
Psalm 88:2
✦
12:00 – 12:30 PM
Midday Prayer
A sacred pause in the day — Midday Prayer and the Angelus renew our union with Christ and His Incarnation.
Midday Rhythm:

Midday Prayer · Angelus · Eucharistic Adoration

"In the middle of the day I will pray and cry aloud: and He shall hear my voice."
Psalm 54:18
🌙
7:00 – 8:30 PM
Evening Prayer
Vespers and Night Prayer bring closure to the day, offering our works to Christ and interceding for the suffering world.
Evening Rhythm:

Vespers · Rosary · Night Prayer (Compline) · Great Silence

"Let my prayer be counted as incense before You, and the lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice."
Psalm 141:2

By celebrating the Divine Office, we unite our voices with Christ, the Eternal High Priest, and with His Body, the Church, offering continuous praise to the Father. In prayer we also intercede for the suffering, the afflicted, and all those in need of consolation, so that no cry of pain is left unheard.

A Guided Spiritual Journey

ACMC Rule of Solitude

Guiding Principles for Silence and Communion with God. Click each principle to enter deeper.

1
Reverence
▾
We enter solitude with humility and awe, recognizing it as a sacred gift from God. In silence, we honor Christ, our Bridegroom, who waits to console us and send us forth as consolers.
2
Simplicity
▾
Our solitude is poor and uncluttered, reflecting the Franciscan spirit. We embrace what is essential: prayer, stillness, Scripture, and creation. In simplicity, our hearts are freed to rest in God alone.
3
Silence
▾
Silence is not emptiness but communion. In silence we listen, we receive, and we allow Christ's consoling voice to shape us. On Fridays, this silence deepens as we fast and intercede for the suffering of the world.
4
Communion
▾
Though alone, we are never isolated. In solitude, we embrace the wounds of humanity and unite them with Christ's Passion. Our hidden prayer becomes an offering of consolation for the Church and the world.
5
Mission
▾
Solitude leads us back to service. On Saturdays, strengthened by silence and fasting, we go forth to the streets, bringing consolation to the homeless and forgotten. Our time apart prepares us for love in action.

Dims distractions for deeper contemplation

Living the Spirit of Anima Christi

ACMC Rule of Consolation

1
Christ-Centered Prayer
Rooted in the Anima Christi, we live in constant communion with Christ, drawing consolation from His pierced Heart.
"Soul of Christ, sanctify me."
2
Compassionate Presence
We listen with tenderness, speak with mercy, and carry the burdens of others with hope.
"Bear one another's burdens." — Gal 6:2
3
Franciscan Simplicity
Following Francis and Clare, we live simply, joyfully, and gratefully — esteeming poverty of spirit as a treasure.
"Blessed are the poor in spirit." — Mt 5:3
4
Silence and Solitude
In silence we hear the voice of the Consoler. Through solitude we are renewed for mission.
"Be still and know that I am God." — Ps 46:10
5
Friday Intercession
On Fridays we unite ourselves to the Passion of Christ through fasting, silence, and intercession for the suffering.
"In His wounds, we find strength."
6
Saturday Mission
On Saturdays, our prayer becomes action — serving the homeless and forgotten, bringing food, presence, and healing love.
"Whatever you do to the least..." — Mt 25:40
7
Fraternal Life
As sisters in Christ, we console one another, bearing weaknesses and rejoicing in one another's gifts.
"Love one another as I have loved you." — Jn 15:12
8
Witness of Hope
We live as lantern-bearers, carrying the light of Christ into places of darkness.
"By His stripes, we are healed." — Is 53:5
Our Mother of Consolation

Devotion to Mary

"If I had an army to say the Rosary, I could conquer the world."
— Blessed Pope Pius IX

As Anima Christi Missionaries of Consolation, we cultivate a deep devotion to Mary, our Mother and model of compassion. We honor her especially under the title Our Lady of Consolation, entrusting ourselves, our community, and our mission to her maternal care and intercession.

Each sister is consecrated to Jesus through Mary, allowing her to lead us ever closer to the pierced Heart of Christ. With Mary, we contemplate the mysteries of our Beloved — His birth, public ministry, Passion and death, and Resurrection — through the regular praying of the Rosary.

We express our devotion to Mary daily, offering her our prayers, works, and presence, and celebrate her feasts with special reverence. Our community also venerates Our Lady of the Annunciation, recognizing her as a guiding mother whose tenderness and courage inspire our missionary outreach.

Pray the Rosary
Click a bead to begin your Rosary.
Larger beads = Our Father · Small beads = Hail Mary
